title: TriPAH: Imbalance-Aware Tri-Prompt Affinity Hashing for Cross-Modal Medical Retrieval
abstract: arXiv:2606.27010v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: In the era of big medical data, efficient cross-modal retrieval is pivotal for evidence-based diagnosis and large-scale case management. Cross-modal medical hashing retrieval aims to enable efficient image-text search and support downstream tasks such as case-based reasoning and decision support by learning compact, semantically aligned binary codes. However, current methods suffer from semantic fragmentation due to noisy clinical language, long-tailed labels, and brittle quantization that weakens alignment. We propose TriPAH, a Tri-Prompt Affinity Hashing framework. TriPAH synthesizes ontology-grounded, patient-level prompts conditioned on normalized clinical cues to yield low-noise textual representations for initial alignment. A lightweight prompt-token mixer performs hierarchical, multi-granularity alignment and produces quantization-ready features under an asymmetric multi-task objective coupling multi-positive contrastive alignment

Title: TriPAH: Imbalance-Aware Tri-Prompt Affinity Hashing for Cross-Modal Medical Retrieval
Abstract: arXiv:2606.27010v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: In the era of big medical data, efficient cross-modal retrieval is pivotal for evidence-based diagnosis and large-scale case management. Cross-modal medical hashing retrieval aims to enable efficient image-text search and support downstream tasks such as case-based reasoning and decision support by learning compact, semantically aligned binary codes. However, current methods suffer from semantic fragmentation due to noisy clinical language, long-tailed labels, and brittle quantization that weakens alignment. We propose TriPAH, a Tri-Prompt Affinity Hashing framework. TriPAH synthesizes ontology-grounded, patient-level prompts conditioned on normalized clinical cues to yield low-noise textual representations for initial alignment. A lightweight prompt-token mixer performs hierarchical, multi-granularity alignment and produces quantization-ready features under an asymmetric multi-task objective coupling multi-positive contrastive alignment
translated_title: TriPAH：面向跨模态医学检索的失衡感知三提示亲和哈希
translated_abstract: 在大医疗数据时代，高效的跨模态检索对于循证诊断和大规模病例管理至关重要。跨模态医学哈希检索旨在通过学习紧凑且语义对齐的二进制编码，实现高效的图像-文本搜索，并支持基于案例的推理和决策支持等下游任务。然而，当前方法因临床语言噪声、长尾标签以及脆弱的量化导致对齐减弱，从而遭受语义碎片化问题。我们提出了TriPAH，一种三提示亲和哈希框架。TriPAH合成了基于本体论的患者级提示，这些提示以标准化临床线索为条件，生成低噪声的文本表示以实现初始对齐。一个轻量级的提示-令牌混合器执行分层、多粒度的对齐，并在一个结合多正例对比对齐的非对称多任务目标下生成适合量化的特征。
tldr: 本文提出TriPAH框架，通过基于本体论的三提示合成和轻量级提示-令牌混合器，有效解决了跨模态医学检索中的语义碎片化、长尾标签和量化脆弱问题。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes the TriPAH framework, which effectively addresses semantic fragmentation, long-tailed labels, and brittle quantization in cross-modal medical retrieval through ontology-grounded tri-prompt synthesis and a lightweight prompt-token mixer.
